zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann Mariah Marshall於1808年1月22日出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯貝維其可 堂史普時，她的父親Nicholas Marshall58歲，母親Mary Caterine53歲。她與Peter Hephner在美國俄亥俄州克林頓特 姆姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她於1860年住在美國威斯康辛州武塔加米且於1880年住在美國堪薩斯州巴頓惠亞特藍德 堂史普。她去世於1888年4月13日在美國威斯康辛州武塔加米麥可維爾享年80歲，埋葬在美國威斯康辛州武塔加米德 特聖 愛德華茲 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ann Mariah Marshall于1808年1月22日出生在美国宾夕法尼亚州亞當斯貝維其可 堂史普时，她的父亲Nicholas Marshall58岁，母亲Mary Caterine53岁。她与Peter Hephner在美国俄亥俄州克林頓特 姆姆结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她于1860年住在美国威斯康辛州武塔加米且于1880年住在美国堪萨斯州巴頓惠亞特藍德 堂史普。她去世于1888年4月13日在美国威斯康辛州武塔加米麥可維爾享年80岁，埋葬在美国威斯康辛州武塔加米德 特聖 愛德華茲 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Mariah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Elaborated spelling of Maria , influenced by the many girls' names of Hebrew origin ending in -a plus an optional final h.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries 1810: Adams, Pennsylvania, United States
War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

American settlers began mining the Wisconsin Territory in the early 1800's. The lead ore in the territory had largely been mined previously by American Indians. By 1829, nearly 4,000 miners had moved to Wisconsin Territory. The miners became known as badgers as they burrowed into hillsides for shelter. The name eventually represented the state and Wisconsin is now known as the Badger State. (Wisconsin Historical Society: Lead Mining in Southwestern Wisconsin)

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Creation of the Republican Party
historicEvents.description: A debate continues over the location of the creation of the Republican Party. Some sources claim that the party was formed in Ripon, Wisconsin, on February 28, 1854. Others claim the first meeting of the Republican Party took place in Jackson, Michigan, on July 6, 1854, where the Republican Party was officially organized. Over 1,000 people were present and candidates were selected for the party, thus making it the first Republican convention.
Historical Boundaries: 1867: Barton, Kansas, United States

historicEvents.title: The Fifteenth Amendment
historicEvents.description: Prohibits the federal government and each state from denying a citizen the right to vote based on that citizen's race, color, or previous condition of servitude. It was the last of the Reconstruction Amendments.

historicEvents.title: First National Strike in U.S. Begins In Pittsburgh Against Pennsylvania Railroad
historicEvents.description: Coming out of an economic crisis, everyone was worried when cuts started happening in the railroad. They went on what would the great railroad strike of 1877.

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Mariah Marshall was born on 22 January 1808, in Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States, her father, Nicholas Marshall, was 58 and her mother, Mary Caterine, was 53. She married Peter Hephner in Center MM, Clinton, Ohio, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. She lived in Outagamie, Wisconsin, United States in 1860 and Wheatland Township, Barton, Kansas, United States in 1880. She died on 13 April 1888, in Mackville, Outagamie, Wisconsin, United States, at the age of 80, and was buried in Saint Edwards Cemetery, Grand Chute, Outagamie, Wisconsin, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ann Mariah Marshall est née le 22 janvier 1808, à Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, son père, Nicholas Marshall, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Mary Caterine, avait 53 ans. Elle a épousé Peter Hephner à Center MM, Clinton, Ohio, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Elle a vécu à Outagamie, Wisconsin, États-Unis en 1860 et à Wheatland Township, Barton, Kansas, États-Unis en 1880. Elle est décédée le 13 avril 1888, à Mackville, Outagamie, Wisconsin, États-Unis, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Edwards Cemetery, Grand Chute, Outagamie, Wisconsin, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ann Mariah Marshall am 22. Januar 1808 in Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Marshall 58 und ihre Mutter Mary Caterine 53. Sie hat in Center MM, Clinton, Ohio, Vereinigte Staaten Peter Hephner geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1860 hat sie in Outagamie, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten und 1880 in Wheatland Township, Barton, Kansas, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 13. April 1888 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Mackville, Outagamie, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Saint Edwards Cemetery, Grand Chute, Outagamie, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Mariah Marshall è nata il il 22 gennaio 1808 nel Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Nicholas Marshall, aveva 58 anni e sua madre, Mary Caterine, aveva 53 anni. Lei sposato Peter Hephner nel Center MM, Clinton, Ohio, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Vissuto a Outagamie, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1860 e nel Wheatland Township, Barton, Kansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1880. Morì il 13 aprile 1888, nel Mackville, Outagamie, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolta a Saint Edwards Cemetery, Grand Chute, Outagamie, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Ann Mariah Marshallが1808年1月22日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アダムズバーウィック トウンシュイップで生まれた時，父Nicholas Marshallは58歳，母Mary Caterineは53歳であった。アメリカ合衆国オハイオ州クリントンセンター ンムでPeter Hephnerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1860年にはアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州オウタガミに，1880年にはアメリカ合衆国カンザス州バートンウィートランド トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼女は1888年4月13日にアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州オウタガミマクヴィルで80歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州オウタガミグランド チウトサイント エドワーズ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Berwick Township, Adams, Pennsylvania, U: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 버윅 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Ann Mariah Marshall(이)가 1808년 1월 22일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 버윅 타운시프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Marshall(은)는 58세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Caterine(은)는 53세였다. 그녀는 미국 오하이오 주 클린턴 세터에서 Peter Hephner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1860년도에 미국 위스콘신 주 우타갬이에서, 1880년도에 미국 캔자스 주 바턴 휘틀랜드 타운시프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1888년 4월 13일 미국 위스콘신 주 우타갬이 맥k빌에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 위스콘신 주 우타갬이 그랜드 c후트 세인트 에드워스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Mariah Marshall nasceu em 22 de janeiro de 1808, em Berwick Township, Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Nicholas Marshall, tinha 58 anos e sua mãe, Mary Caterine, tinha 53 anos. Ela casou-se com Peter Hephner em Center MM, Clinton, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela viveu em Outagamie, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América em 1860 e em Wheatland Township, Barton, Cansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1880. Ela faleceu em 13 de abril de 1888, em Mackville, Outagamie, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América, com 80 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint Edwards Cemetery, Grand Chute, Outagamie, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Wheatland, Barton, Kansas, United States: уитланд тоуншип, бартон, Канзас, Соединенные штаты

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ann Mariah Marshall родилась 22 января 1808 г. в местности бирвикк тоуншип, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Nicholas Marshall было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Caterine, 53. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Peter Hephner в местности кентир мм, клинтон, Огайо, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она жила в местности оутагэими, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году и в местности уитланд тоуншип, бартон, Канзас, Соединенные штаты в 1880 году. Она умерла 13 апреля 1888 г. в местности макквилл, оутагэими, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт эдвардс кеметири, гранд чут, оутагэими, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты.
